result.stdout:
Description:
  Janitor - Refactor and standardize C# repository structure

Usage:
  Janitor.Cli [command] [options]

Options:
  --version       Show version information
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  init <output-path>      Create a configuration file interactively [default: .janitorrc.json]
  plan <repo-path>        Show what changes would be made without applying them [default: .]
  apply <repo-path>       Apply transformations to repository [default: .]
  template                Manage configuration templates
  validate <config-path>  Validate configuration file
  backup                  Manage repository backups
  version                 Show version information
  templates               List available templates (alias for 'template list')

commands.payload:
Description:
  Janitor - Refactor and standardize C# repository structure

Usage:
  Janitor.Cli [command] [options]

Options:
  --version       Show version information
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  init <output-path>      Create a configuration file interactively [default: .janitorrc.json]
  plan <repo-path>        Show what changes would be made without applying them [default: .]
  apply <repo-path>       Apply transformations to repository [default: .]
  template                Manage configuration templates
  validate <config-path>  Validate configuration file
  backup                  Manage repository backups
  version                 Show version information
  templates               List available templates (alias for 'template list')

result.stdout:
Description:
  Apply transformations to repository

Usage:
  Janitor.Cli apply [<repo-path>] [options]

Arguments:
  <repo-path>  Path to the repository to organize [default: .]

Options:
  -c, --config <config>  Path to configuration file
  -y, --yes              Automatically approve all proposals (non-interactive mode)
  -?, -h, --help         Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Apply transformations to repository

Usage:
  Janitor.Cli apply [<repo-path>] [options]

Arguments:
  <repo-path>  Path to the repository to organize [default: .]

Options:
  -c, --config <config>  Path to configuration file
  -y, --yes              Automatically approve all proposals (non-interactive mode)
  -?, -h, --help         Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Manage repository backups

Usage:
  Janitor.Cli backup [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  restore <backup-path> <target-repo>  Restore repository from backup [default: .]
  list <repo-path>                     List available backups for a repository [default: .]

  list <repo-path>                     List available backups for a repository [default: .]

result.stdout:
Description:
  List available backups for a repository

Usage:
  Janitor.Cli backup list [<repo-path>] [options]

Arguments:
  <repo-path>  Path to repository [default: .]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Restore repository from backup

Usage:
  Janitor.Cli backup restore <backup-path> [<target-repo>] [options]

Arguments:
  <backup-path>  Path to the backup folder
  <target-repo>  Path to restore to (defaults to current directory) [default: .]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

commands.payload:

Usage:
  Janitor.Cli backup restore <backup-path> [<target-repo>] [options]

Arguments:
  <backup-path>  Path to the backup folder
  <target-repo>  Path to restore to (defaults to current directory) [default: .]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Create a configuration file interactively

Usage:
  Janitor.Cli init [<output-path>] [options]

Arguments:
  <output-path>  Path where config file will be created (optional, defaults to .janitorrc.json) [default: .janitorrc.json]

Options:
  -y, --yes                  Generate config with default values without prompting
  -t, --template <template>  Template to use (dotnet-standard, dotnet-clean-architecture, dotnet-vertical-slice, dotnet-microservice, dotnet-minimal)
  -o, --output <output>      Explicit output path for config file
  -?, -h, --help             Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Create a configuration file interactively

Usage:
  Janitor.Cli init [<output-path>] [options]

Arguments:
  <output-path>  Path where config file will be created (optional, defaults to .janitorrc.json) [default: .janitorrc.json]

Options:
  -y, --yes                  Generate config with default values without prompting
  -t, --template <template>  Template to use (dotnet-standard, dotnet-clean-architecture, dotnet-vertical-slice, dotnet-microservice, dotnet-minimal)
  -o, --output <output>      Explicit output path for config file
  -?, -h, --help             Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Show what changes would be made without applying them

Usage:
  Janitor.Cli plan [<repo-path>] [options]

Arguments:
  <repo-path>  Path to the repository to analyze [default: .]

Options:
  -c, --config <config>  Path to configuration file
  -f, --format <format>  Output format (console, json, markdown) [default: console]
  -?, -h, --help         Show help and usage information
